page: Microsoft Azure L8as_v4 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Lasv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 1920 GB of Premium Storage-capable capacity. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the L8as_v4 demonstrates strong single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 3078.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 2080.0, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12313.93 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8101.0. Memory performance shows a significant tradeoff: while cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth is average at 607.39 GB/sec, large-block uncached read-only memory bandwidth is weak at 38.85 GB/sec. Text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression perform strongly, while database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput fall within average ranges. LLM inference speeds are average for small models but drop to weak levels for medium-sized models during prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L8as_v4 provides a high density of local storage and memory relative to its 8 vCPUs, making it a resource-dense option for workloads that do not require massive multi-core scaling. The combination of 64.0 GB of RAM and 1920 GB of local storage makes this instance well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive applications. It is also appropriate for general-purpose workloads, text processing, and software compilation, though it is less optimal for large-scale parallel computing or memory-intensive uncached streaming operations due to the observed bandwidth bottlenecks.
description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Lasv4 family, powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 64.0 GB of system memory and 1920 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage, with no GPU. The instance exhibits strong single-core CPU capabilities, scoring in the top 25% on stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside strong text processing and single-threaded decompression performance. However, multi-core performance and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth are average to weak. Given its high storage-to-vCPU and memory-to-vCPU ratios, the L8as_v4 is highly suitable for database operations, caching systems, and memory-intensive workloads that benefit from fast local storage without requiring extreme multi-threaded compute scaling.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v4 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and 1920 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8as_v4 features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine offering high local storage capacity and balanced memory density for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and 1920 GB of local storage.
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance.
Exhibits average multi-core performance and weak uncached memory read bandwidth.
Best suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications.
